evals.prompt: Session is starting. Load any existing state and memory from .autonomis/ that's relevant to the current project (we're working on the auth feature). If nothing exists yet, create the .autonomis layout (state, memory, runs, research) and say what you loaded or created.
evals.expected_output: Read from .autonomis/state/ and .autonomis/memory/ (or create dirs if missing). Report what was loaded; if creating, use mkdir and Write. Selective loading: only auth-related scope if filtering.
expectations: Output references .autonomis/state or .autonomis/memory or reports what was loaded/created
Output is non-empty and describes state load, update, or directory creation

evals.prompt: We just finished the login work unit. Update .autonomis/state/ with the current phase and next steps. Use Edit on existing files with stable anchors; then Read back to verify. Don't overwrite unrelated sections.
evals.expected_output: Edit applied to state file(s) under .autonomis/state/; Read performed to verify. Stable section anchors used; no rename of existing headers.
expectations: Output references .autonomis/state or .autonomis/memory or reports what was loaded/created
Output is non-empty and describes state load, update, or directory creation
